trans-unit.source: Projects and Folder Structure
trans-unit.target: Projects and Folder Structure

trans-unit.source: __🔙[Back to Homepage](/content/intro.md)__
trans-unit.target: __🔙[Back to Homepage](/content/intro.md)__

trans-unit.source: This wiki article covers the best practices for the creation and management of geodata and QGIS projects.
trans-unit.target: This wiki article covers the best practices for the creation and management of geodata and QGIS projects.

trans-unit.source: Step-by-step: Setting up a new QGIS project from scratch
trans-unit.target: Step-by-step: Setting up a new QGIS project from scratch

trans-unit.source: Copy the standard folder structure for QGIS projects to the place you want to store your whole project. You can download the standard folder structure *here*.
trans-unit.target: Copy the standard folder structure for QGIS projects to the place you want to store your whole project. You can download the standard folder structure *here*.

trans-unit.source: Open QGIS and create a new project. Click on `Project` -> `New Project`
trans-unit.target: Open QGIS and create a new project. Click on `Project` -> `New Project`

trans-unit.source: Create a new QGIS Project
trans-unit.target: Create a new QGIS Project

trans-unit.source: Save the new project in the `Project` folder in the standard folder structure and git push.
trans-unit.target: Save the new project in the `Project` folder in the standard folder structure and git push.

trans-unit.source: Give your project a name and click `Save`. :::{Tip} Do not use spaces ` ` in the name, instead always use underscores `_`. :::
trans-unit.target: Give your project a name and click `Save`. :::{Tip} Do not use spaces ` ` in the name, instead always use underscores `_`. :::

trans-unit.source: Save Project
trans-unit.target: Save Project

trans-unit.source: Check the Coordinate Reference System (CRS)/EPSG code of the project to the CRS/EPSG you want to use. For more information check the wiki article on [Projection](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_projections_wiki.html#how-to-check-epsg-code-crs-of-your-qgis-project-and-change-it).
trans-unit.target: Check the Coordinate Reference System (CRS)/EPSG code of the project to the CRS/EPSG you want to use. For more information check the wiki article on [Projection](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_projections_wiki.html#how-to-check-epsg-code-crs-of-your-qgis-project-and-change-it).

trans-unit.source: Check and change CRS/EPSG
trans-unit.target: Check and change CRS/EPSG

trans-unit.source: :::{Tip} The layer data used in the project are not saved in the project file. Instead, the project file only contains the file paths where the layer data were located at the time the project was last saved on the PC. If the location of this layer data is subsequently changed, the error message "handle unavailable layers" will appear when the project is opened again. Good data organisation with a fixed and well thought-out folder structure prevents such problems. :::
trans-unit.target: :::{Tip} The layer data used in the project are not saved in the project file. Instead, the project file only contains the file paths where the layer data were located at the time the project was last saved on the PC. If the location of this layer data is subsequently changed, the error message "handle unavailable layers" will appear when the project is opened again. Good data organisation with a fixed and well thought-out folder structure prevents such problems. :::

trans-unit.source: Open existing QGIS Projects
trans-unit.target: Open existing QGIS Projects

trans-unit.source: Open QGIS → `Project` → `Open` → Select your project
trans-unit.target: Open QGIS → `Project` → `Open` → Select your project

trans-unit.source: __Open QGIS Project__
trans-unit.target: __Open QGIS Project__

trans-unit.source: Standard Folder Structure
trans-unit.target: Standard Folder Structure

trans-unit.source: The standard folder structure has two principal advantages:
trans-unit.target: The standard folder structure has two principal advantages:

trans-unit.source: By sharing the whole project folder, we can be certain that the project will run without problems on a different computer.
trans-unit.target: By sharing the whole project folder, we can be certain that the project will run without problems on a different computer.

trans-unit.source: The folder structure supports the proper organization of geodata and supports the stable function of a QGIS project.
trans-unit.target: The folder structure supports the proper organization of geodata and supports the stable function of a QGIS project.

trans-unit.source: The folder structure template can be downloaded [__here__](https://github.com/GIScience/gis-training-resource-center/blob/main/fig/GIS_Project_folder_template.zip).
trans-unit.target: The folder structure template can be downloaded [__here__](https://github.com/GIScience/gis-training-resource-center/blob/main/fig/GIS_Project_folder_template.zip).

trans-unit.source: Standard folder structure. Source: HeiGIT :::
trans-unit.target: Standard folder structure. Source: HeiGIT :::
